name: View Transitions ŌĆö Next.js
description: Enable `experimental: { viewTransition: true }` in `next.config` to wrap `<Link>` navigations. Prefer `<Link transitionTypes={[...]}>` when available (Next 15+ canary / 16+); grep `node_modules/next/dist` if unsure ŌĆö else `startTransition`+`addTransitionType`+`router.push`. Do not add `react@canary` manually on Next App Router. Avoid layout-level VT around `{children}` if pages define VT (nested enter/exit won't fire). `loading.tsx` = implicit Suspense: VT skeleton exit in `loading.tsx`, VT content enter in page. Same-route dynamic segment: `key`+`name`+`share` inside Suspense. Server Components: `<ViewTransition>` and typed `<Link>` ok; programmatic types need client.

metadata.intent: Integrate view transitions idiomatically with the App Router

must_use: Feature flag for link-driven VT when using Next integration
must_not: Layout VT + page VT that both expect enter/exit without checking nesting rules
conventions: Use `router.replace` inside `startTransition` for server-driven filter/sort URL updates to animate list identity.

steps.description: Turn on `experimental.viewTransition` and verify Next version supports `transitionTypes` on `Link` before relying on it.
Mirror explicit Suspense VT pattern using `loading.tsx` for skeleton exit + page content enter.
For changing dynamic params without route remount, combine `key={segment}` with named `share` VT to force a paired transition.
